heading: Confidentiality privileges relating to taxpayer communications
label: 7525 Confidentiality privileges relating to taxpayer communications
notes: Amendments2004—Subsec. (b). amended heading and text of subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The privilege under subsection (a) shall not apply to any written communication between a federally authorized tax practitioner and a director, shareholder, officer, or employee, agent, or representative of a corporation in connection with the promotion of the direct or indirect participation of such corporation in any tax shelter (as defined in section 6662(d)(2)(C)(iii)).”Pub. L. 108–357

Effective Date of 2004 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–357, title VIII, § 813(b)118 Stat. 1581Oct. 22, 2004“The amendment made by this section [amending this section] shall apply to communications made on or after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”Oct. 22, 2004

Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–206, title III, § 3411(c)112 Stat. 751July 22, 1998“The amendments made by this section [enacting this section] shall apply to communications made on or after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”July 22, 1998

children.heading: Uniform application to taxpayer communications with federally authorized practitioners
children.label: a Uniform application to taxpayer communications with federally authorized practitioners

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: With respect to tax advice, the same common law protections of confidentiality which apply to a communication between a taxpayer and an attorney shall also apply to a communication between a taxpayer and any federally authorized tax practitioner to the extent the communication would be considered a privileged communication if it were between a taxpayer and an attorney.

children.label: A
children.text: any noncriminal tax matter before the Internal Revenue Service; and

children.label: B
children.text: any noncriminal tax proceeding in Federal court brought by or against the United States.

children.heading: Federally authorized tax practitioner
children.label: A Federally authorized tax practitioner
children.text: The term “federally authorized tax practitioner” means any individual who is authorized under Federal law to practice before the Internal Revenue Service if such practice is subject to Federal regulation under , United States Code.section 330 of title 31

children.heading: Tax advice
children.label: B Tax advice
children.text: The term “tax advice” means advice given by an individual with respect to a matter which is within the scope of the individual’s authority to practice described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Section not to apply to communications regarding tax shelters
children.label: b Section not to apply to communications regarding tax shelters
B
children.text: any director, officer, employee, agent, or representative of the person, or

children.label: C
children.text: any other person holding a capital or profits interest in the person, and
in connection with the promotion of the direct or indirect participation of the person in any tax shelter (as defined in section 6662(d)(2)(C)(ii)).
